hints.text: All car coverage = **Gen4**; **bollard roughly every 50 m** (B&W, white/grey reflectors, orange at intersections)
German: **Ä Ö Ü ß**; Luxembourg has near-identical bollards but **3 bolts** (Germany = 2)
**Yellow town-entry signs** (black text, metal frame) = town / municipality / county
km markers: **blue = Autobahn (A-road)**, white w/ black border = B/local; black/dark **sign backs** (also IT/RO/AL)
**Wind turbines** far more common than anywhere else in Europe; green windshield sticker ("4")
Elevation: NW very flat · centre hilly · **south = Alps** (Austrian border)
Brick houses → NW; thatched roofs + rock walls → Schleswig-Holstein / N. Frisian islands; vineyards → SW
White **3-section road markers** + round wooden bollards; black-disk bollard bases → all of eastern Germany
Roadside post styles differ by state (rocky unpainted → Rhineland-Palatinate/Saxony; tall white → Saarland/Thuringia)
